title: What an American thinks about Slovenia

transcript.text: Hello, this video is what I think about, is what an American thinks about Slovenia.
It's late, I'm tired, hopefully I can do this.
I was only there a couple of days, but it was a country that I went through.
It's very pretty, Ljubljana, the capital of Slovenia, is very pretty.
It's in a big valley surrounded by mountains at 360 degrees.
And there's this river going right through the middle of it, and any city with a river coming through the middle of it with views of mountains is pretty.
And there's this big hill in the middle of the city next to the river.
And it's a college town, I went to try to look for a place to stay on campus, and could not find a place there.
It was like abandoned, or not many people, I guess it was like a holiday or something.
But then I asked these girls on the street where like a hotel would be or something, so they showed me a place.
And then they invited to hang out with me the next day, so that was cool.
So yes, I would say, Slovenians are very nice.
And they gave me a tour, and we walked around, and they were like, oh yeah, we remember a few years ago, and there was the war.
Because that's like Yugoslavia, Slovenia, I guess it's part of Yugoslavia.
They had a war a few years ago, beforehand.
I was there in 97.
And they were like, yeah, we were talking, and somebody was like, yeah, there's a war.
We're at war, we saw the planes go by, oh wow.
So anyways, but they're nice.
It had a quaint feel to it, like an old, you know, very old city.
You know, peaceful place.
People seemed happy there.
Seemed like it had pretty good infrastructure to it.
People looked nice.
Just white.
People, normal looking people.
I didn't see any gypsies there.
I'm pretty tall there, too.
Yeah, that's cool.
The girl I was hanging out with was taller than me.
This other girl I was asking what direction I was with was, too.
And then there's this other.
So, yeah.
I heard there's even some skewers which are on there, some, too.
But anyways, that's what I think about Slovenia.
Just passed through.
It's a pretty cool place.
